analysis.questions_missed: Q11 (essay questions consistently scoring 70-75%), Q3 and Q9 (multiple choice questions on conjunctions and transitions)
analysis.error_analysis: The student consistently struggles with two key areas: selecting appropriate conjunctions and transitions in multiple choice questions, and developing fully structured expository essays. The essay responses show incomplete development, earning only 70-75% despite following the basic format requirements. The multiple choice errors specifically involve logical connectors between sentences and ideas.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot effectively identify and use logical conjunctions and transitional phrases to connect contrasting or complementary ideas. Writes essays that meet basic structural requirements but lack sufficient depth, detail, or textual evidence to earn full points. Rushes through all tests (14-21 minutes), suggesting inadequate time allocation for essay planning and revision.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign targeted practice on conjunctions and transitions, focusing on identifying relationships between ideas (contrast, addition, cause-effect). Practice timed essay writing with emphasis on developing body paragraphs with specific examples and textual evidence. Implement time management strategies, requiring minimum 25-30 minutes per test with designated planning time before writing essays.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Animated movies teach more than just an entertaining story. These movies can teach life lessons, such as standing up for oneself or understanding peoples true emotions. Now, it is time to tell you how these movies do more then just entertainment.




Lets start with describing animated movies. Animated movies are a type of entertainment source that many children watch almost every day. These movies provide many life lessons along the way, which can include teaching kids math or learning what to do correctly. 




Next, the life lessons it can teach you. These movies provide lessons that can be very useful in life, such as courage and bravery. An example is Matilda, as she refuses to stop what she loves the most, archery. She doesn't follow strict rules everybody provides her, and she stands up for what she likes. This example shows that you do not need to follow strict expectations, you can follow your own path and stand up for what is right.




Lastly, these life lessons you can apply to real life. For example, if you watch a movie about giving your friends one more chance, you can apply that to your friends as well. Everybody deserves a second chance, even if what they did may have broken the friendship. Another example is that after you watch a show about standing up for oneself, you can apply that to yourself. This makes you want to stand up to what you believe is the right choice, not what others think.




Animated movies do more than just be an entertainment source. They can teach us life lessons, such as protecting what is right and giving others a second chance to change. These movies do more than you originally thought.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
Monarch butterflies do more than we think, but they are dwindling slowly. We can help protect them in many ways, and one of them includes building way station. Now, I will get into more detail of how we need to help keep these beautiful butterflies thriving.




Every fall, thousands of monarch butterflies fly hundreds of miles from the United States all the way to the mountains of Mexico. When they arrive there, they spend majority of the winter resting in trees. However, when spring arrives, new generations start the journey, stopping to refuel and lay eggs. But there are some problems that have been affecting these butterflies. One of the problems include people clearing land to be used for farms and lawns, which have been clearing out a plant that the monarch butterflies depend on, milkweed.




Destorying milkweed has a huge affect on the monarach butterflies. This plant is a crucial food source for these butterflies, and the reduced amount of these plants is lowering the survival of the monarch butterfly. Another problem that heavily affects these butterflies are huge storms wiping out the trees they normally rest on during winter season. These problems affect the butterfly by reducing the amount of butterflies that are able to migrate back to the United States.




However, we can do a lot to protect these butterflies. One of the ways we have helped is by tagging these butterflies with small stickers. This includes counting the caterpilliars in the area, and record monarch sightings online. By doing this, we have helped scientists understands the well being of the butterflies and where they need the most protection.




We need to ensure that these butterflies stay thriving. The main problems were removal of milkweed and storms wiping out resting trees, and this affects the amount of butterflies that come back to the United States for spring. However, we have done something to help, which includes recording monarch sightings online. What we do for the monarch butterflies will help ensure the survival of these magnificent butterflies.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how students learn new languages in effective ways. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
Learning a second language is benefical. It helps students, especially younger children, learn a language they couldn't speak before. Now, lets go to why learning a second language is crucial.




First, learning a second language has its benefits. Various studies show that it can help strengthen memory and improve solving skills. Also, scientists say that younger children learn new sounds easier because their brains are in the developing stage. This concludes that learning a second language at a young age can help build these skills.




One way that young children can learn a second language is by immersion. In immersion classes, the instructor speaks the new language every day. Students hear them all day, such as during learns, group work, or even small requests. Because of being exposed to the language, young students pick up the new words easier. Also, they learn grammar by paying close attention to how the languages sounds in real life. 




But, some people believe that older students learn better in organized lessons. Others think that younger children learn more naturally because they copy sounds and accents easier. Though it may be true the older students can learn better, it is better for them to learn while they are at a young age.




No matter the way that people teach them, learning a new language can open various doors. It helps kids communicate with others and explore the world with a new perspective. Learning how to speak another language is benifical for their growth.

skills.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details
Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.error_analysis: Ariel consistently struggles with extended paragraph writing (Q11), producing single sentences or very brief responses instead of well-organized paragraphs that develop a central idea with supporting details. She also has difficulty with sentence transformation tasks, particularly converting statements to questions (missing "Were" in "Where the books sold...") and combining sentences using specific grammatical structures like appositives and conjunctions.
analysis.root_causes: Inability to develop extended written responses beyond single sentences - all Q11 paragraph prompts result in 1-2 sentence answers lacking organization and detail development. Confusion with question formation and auxiliary verbs ("Where the books sold" instead of "Were the books sold"). Persistent spelling errors with common words ("teaddy" for "teddy", "lesen" for "listen", "inportant" for "important") that suggest phonetic spelling rather than visual word recognition.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign paragraph development exercises using graphic organizers to practice writing 4-5 sentence responses with topic sentences and supporting details before attempting Q11-style prompts. Focus on question formation drills specifically targeting auxiliary verbs (do/does/did, was/were, can/could) with sentence transformation practice. Implement structured spelling practice targeting high-frequency words and common phonetic patterns, particularly double letters and silent letters.

mastered_in_alphawrite_not_tests.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.error_analysis: Armaan consistently struggles with selecting appropriate conjunctions and transition words in multiple choice questions, indicating weak understanding of logical connectors between ideas. His essay responses show incomplete development - he either provides minimal content (coded as "$2b") or writes fragments that cut off mid-sentence, suggesting he cannot sustain extended writing within time constraints.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot identify and use appropriate conjunctions and transitions to connect ideas logically in sentences and paragraphs. Fails to complete full expository essay structure consistently, either producing minimal responses or incomplete fragments that don't meet the required introduction-body-conclusion format. Rushes through all assessments (14-22 minutes), preventing adequate time for essay planning and development.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign targeted practice with conjunction and transition word identification through structured sentence-combining exercises before retesting. Implement timed writing practice with scaffolded essay templates to build stamina and organizational skills for 5-paragraph expository format. Require 30-minute minimum testing sessions with mandatory planning time to address rushing behavior that undermines essay completion.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Animated movies are exciting to watch. But animated movies can also teach valuable life lessons like fixing mistakes, adapting to change, forgiving others, and learning about people and places around the world.




In many animated movies, characters sometimes have a big change that happens or make mistakes. They go on long journeys to try to fix what they did wrong. Children can learn that it's okay to make mistakes as long as you try to fix them and grow.




Animated movies can also show children how to forgive others. For example in the movie Dispicable Me, the main character Gru makes a mistake and betrays the trust of his 3 daughters but throughout the movie he tries to make it up to them.




Finally, animated movies can show different places and people around the globe. This helps children learn about the world and become more kind to others.




In conclusion, animated movies can teach valuable life lessons to children and help them grow. Animated movies are exciting to watch and are also a helpful teaching tool.2c:T8b6,

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
Every fall, millions of monarch butterflies fly thousands of miles from Canada and the USA to Mexico so that they can rest in trees during the winter.


During the spring they journey north again laying eggs along the way. However more butterflies have died during this journey than ever before.


Because people have been destroying milkweed plants that the butterflies need to survive the monarch population is decreasing. People have now realized about this and they have became citizen scientists.


People tag butterflies, count caterpillars, and when they see a monarch butterfly they can tell scientists online. This helps scientists know where butterflies need help and where butterflies are thriving.


In conclusion people can help monarch butterflies by tagging them online and planting more milkweed plants.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how students learn new languages in effective ways. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
Students learn new languages every day in many various ways, for example some learn from immersion classes, some learn from speaking with friends, and some learn with writing to pen pals in other countries.




One way students learn a new language is by taking immersion classes. In the class, the teacher gives the students different work that involves only using the new language. In some immersion classes, students are only allowed to speak in the new language to help them learn faster.




Another way students learn new languages is from talking to friends. Some students have friends from other countries that know the native language there. Their friend can then explain how to speak the language and tutor them too.




Finally, students can learn new languages from writing to pen pals. Some classes can connect students to other students in a different countries so that they can be each others pen pal. They write each other about their lives and can ask each other questions too. They can also learn by writing their letters in the new language.




In conclusion, learning a new language can be easier than students think. They just need to find the right method.2c:Td9d,

incorrect_questions.student_answer:
Students learn new languages every day in many various ways, for example some learn from immersion classes, some learn from speaking with friends, and some learn with writing to pen pals in other countries.




One way students learn a new language is by taking immersion classes. In the class, the teacher gives the students different work that involves only using the new language. In some immersion classes, students are only allowed to speak in the new language to help them learn faster.




Another way students learn new languages is from talking to friends. Some students have friends from other countries that know the native language there. Their friend can then explain how to speak the language and tutor them too.




Finally, students can learn new languages from writing to pen pals. Some classes can connect students to other students in a different countries so that they can be each others pen pal. They write each other about their lives and can ask each other questions too. They can also learn by writing their letters in the new language.




In conclusion, learning a new language can be easier than students think. They just need to find the right method.2c:Td9d,

questions.title: Extended Text Question
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how teamwork helped restore the Mithi River and what the community learned from this experience. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : U
questions.student_answer: For many years, the Mithi River in Mumbai, India, was full of trash. Factories were dumping waste into the water which resulted in the river turning black. The river was always dirty until a group called Earth5R decided to help. They wanted to make the river clean again but they knew they would need help. First, they tested where the problems were the worst and mapped them out. Next, they asked people to help them and invited families, schools, and local shops. After a few people joined the word started to spread and more people had joined in. After a few months of hard work and teamwork, the group had removed over fourteen tonnes of trash. Consequently, Earth5R worked with scientists and planted grasses along the banks. The plants helped stop soil from washing away and helped clean the water. They also recycled the plastic they found in the river and turned the food scraps into compost. Over time, the river started to look cleaner and smell better. Tests showed the water was twenty percent cleaner than when they started the project. In conclusion, the community had learned that instead of ignoring the problem they needed to take action. Now even locals hold cleanups for the river to keep it clean for the future.2c:Ta9c,

analysis.error_analysis: Armin shows a clear pattern of providing nonsense answers ("asdq", "ksjsjss", "asdasd") when rushed or overwhelmed, particularly in early tests. When he does attempt genuine responses, he struggles with sentence mechanics (missing punctuation, incorrect grammar) and fails to develop complete thoughts. His paragraph writing (Q11) consistently lacks organization, development, and fails to address prompts fully, even when he demonstrates understanding of basic concepts.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot construct well-organized paragraphs with topic sentences, supporting details, and conclusions - instead writes single sentences or brief, underdeveloped responses. Lacks understanding of sentence types and transformations (declarative to interrogative, creating appositives, combining sentences with conjunctions). Gives up quickly under time pressure, resulting in random keystrokes rather than attempted answers.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Paragraph Structure KGs focusing on topic sentences, supporting details, and conclusions with explicit templates and guided practice. Practice sentence transformation exercises (declarative to interrogative, appositive construction, conjunction usage) through targeted grammar activities. Implement untimed practice sessions to build confidence before attempting timed assessments, focusing on completing one quality response rather than rushing through all questions.

skills.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

mastered_in_alphawrite_not_tests.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.error_analysis: Atticus consistently struggles with sentence transformation tasks, particularly converting statements to questions (changes plural to singular, omits words) and combining sentences with specific conjunctions (uses wrong conjunctions or creates grammatically incorrect structures). His paragraph responses lack proper organization and development, often missing key content requirements while containing multiple spelling errors and incomplete thoughts.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot accurately manipulate sentence structures when given specific grammatical requirements (subordinating conjunctions, question formation, appositives). Lacks paragraph development skills - writes brief, loosely connected sentences rather than well-organized paragraphs with clear topic sentences and supporting details. Demonstrates inconsistent spelling patterns and frequently rushes through assessments without adequate review time.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Grammar G3-G4 KGs focusing specifically on sentence combining with conjunctions and statement-to-question transformations with guided practice. Practice structured paragraph writing using graphic organizers to develop topic sentences and supporting details before attempting timed assessments. Implement spelling intervention targeting frequently misspelled words (because, their, carefully) and establish time management strategies to reduce rushing behavior.

analysis.error_analysis: Ben consistently struggles with sentence combining and transformation tasks (Q2, Q3), often using incorrect conjunctions or failing to follow specific instructions like using required subordinating conjunctions. His spelling errors are frequent and systematic ("mountants," "disition," "evenchualy," "meat" for "meet"), and he demonstrates difficulty with paragraph writing, producing underdeveloped responses that lack proper organization and detail.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot accurately combine sentences using specified grammatical structures (subordinating conjunctions, appositives); consistently misspells common words indicating weak phonetic awareness and spelling patterns; writes paragraph responses that lack sufficient development, evidence, and proper sentence variety for grade-level expectations.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G3-G4 KGs focusing specifically on subordinating conjunctions, appositives, and sentence combining techniques with guided practice. Implement systematic spelling instruction targeting frequently misspelled words and phonetic patterns through daily spelling activities. Practice structured paragraph writing using graphic organizers and sentence starters before attempting full writing assessments.

analysis.error_analysis: Blake consistently struggles with sentence fragments, failing to convert them into complete sentences by adding proper subjects or predicates (Q2 across multiple tests). He also has a recurring issue with the extended paragraph writing task (Q11), showing "$31" as responses, suggesting technical difficulties or inability to complete longer written responses within the time limit.
analysis.root_causes: Incomplete understanding of sentence structure, specifically identifying and correcting fragments by adding missing sentence components; inability to complete or properly submit extended writing responses, possibly due to technical issues with the testing platform or time management difficulties with longer tasks; frequent spelling errors and punctuation mistakes that indicate weak proofreading skills.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentence Structure G4 KGs focusing specifically on identifying and correcting sentence fragments through guided practice; Provide technical support training and practice with the testing platform to resolve the recurring "$31" submission issue on paragraph responses; Implement timed writing practice sessions with shorter paragraph prompts to build stamina and completion skills before attempting full-length responses.

questions.student_answer: At the kickoff, Cody lines himself with Muds postiton, he thought that his friend wouldn't be intrested that much. Cody thinks that he can jump in and help his team if the other corners Mud. Ryan swiftly kicked the ball across the entire filed to Amanda. Cody had always been puzzled why Amanda plays with the boys' team. He didn't have to think for too long. Amanda dodged a defender with impressive control. Amanda got ready and used her whole body to block the ball from one of the opponents. After a little while, Amanda was fully swarmed. There was no exit, Amanda made a split-second decision and kicked the ball at Mud. Cody was scared assuming that made was going to mess up. The enemy team ran at Mud, not thinking that they would get outplayed. Something no one expected to happen, happened. Mud swifty dodged Mitch and Hans and Mud started runing down field. Cody was in shock. Since when was Mud this speedy. Manhender tried to block Mud, but Mud brushed past him like it was nothing. Mitch tried to get the ball, but Mud adapted and with all his force unleashed a huge kick. The ball whooshed past the goalie and made it into the net. Blue Team had finally scored their first goal. Blue team swarmed Mud, screaming and giving high-fives. Cody, still can't prosess what just happened, spent the rest of the game trying to get the ball but never made it.32:T2c10,

questions.title: Essay with Plain Text
questions.prompt: Read the article "Are People Ready for Robots?" Based on the information in the article, write  in response to the following: one well-organized paragraph  Explain some ways humans benefit from allowing robots to complete simple tasks at home and in the workplace.  Your paragraph should:  - clearly state your thesis  - organize your writing  - develop your ideas in detail  - use evidence from the selection in your response  - use correct spelling, capitalization, punctuation, and grammar  Make s
questions.student_answer: When you hear robots, what do you think about? Maybe books or shows. Well robots are here now. Now you can't go one day without seeing something that is robotic. People make robots sound scary and dangerous. Actually robots help so many people. Some jobs have robots and people work together. One factory located in the Netherlands. Humans use robot arms to fix machines and put them together. Using the useful parts of robots workers can make many more products than they could with their human arms. Located in Pittsburgh, Carneigie Mellon University is having robots do small jobs. Bring papers or help people find where they're going. These robots are basically an Ipad on wheels. They go everywhere they need to go even going into elevators. Still, people need to tell them where to go. In Chicago, Hotel EMC2 have 2 robots Cleo and Leo. They bring people items and they even make deliveries. Again, humans still have to tell them what to do, but they are much faster and cooler. You can also put on a robot? Exoskeletons, metal frames with motors, make moving heavy stuff more easy and safe. The exoskeleton adds strength to move items. Exoskeletons also help disabled people move not fully, but getiing there. Robots can also help you in your house. Robo pets are also a thing of the future. Robo dogs don't need as much care as a dog, but can also still help comfort people. Robots are joining society in lots of places. They still help and commands, but will help so many people.

questions.title: Essay with Plain Text
questions.prompt: Read the article "Trees Rise in the Desert" Based on the information in the article, write  in response to the following: one well-organized paragraph  How did Yacouba’s  help him find a way to grow plants in dry land? Use  to support your answer. ideas and character evidence from the article  Your paragraph should: use evidence from the selection in your response use correct spelling, capitalization, punctuation, and grammar  Make sure to: review the selection plan your response write your resp
questions.student_answer: People laughed when they saw someone digging small holes, it was farmer Yacouba Sawadog. On average each hole was eight inches deep and a foot wide. Yacouba threw many things in the hole such as leaves, manure, and other junk. After all of that he planted seeds? Someone shouted at him " Do you think those seeds will actually grow without water?" Burkina Faso, 1980, a country located in Africa, had no good rain for at least a decade. The country is between the Sahara Desert and rain forests. The soil was cracking, dead, dry, and slowy it was becoming as bad the Sahara. This mainly happens when too many people and animals live there. Yocouba told everyone rain would come back. When the ground gets ro har water slips away. But the holes that Yacouba made would grab and keep the rain. All the stuff that Yacouba put in the hole would make the plants grow and make the soil stay wet for longer. Yacouba believed that he could turn the land green again. He said people have done this a long time ago, and it worked. People still doubted Yacouba. I am happy to say this Yacouba was right. Right now, amazing green trees grow where a desert had once took over. Yacouba idea had spread through West Africa making many farmers start to use the holes and other ways to save water. Yacouba now travels to show people how bring life to very dry places. He still believes that there is hope for the amazing future.

skills.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.questions_missed: Q11 (extended paragraph responses), Q2/Q4 (grammar mechanics), Q5-Q9 (single-sentence explanatory responses)
analysis.error_analysis: Connor consistently struggles with extended paragraph writing, producing single sentences or underdeveloped responses when multi-sentence paragraphs are required (Q11 across all tests). He also demonstrates specific grammar weaknesses, including inability to follow conjunction instructions (used "so" instead of "since" in Q2) and frequent spelling errors ("momment," "outdoars," "board" for "bored," "excitment"). His single-sentence responses often lack clarity and contain awkward phrasing or inappropriate word choices.
analysis.root_causes: Inadequate paragraph development skills - writes single sentences when full paragraphs with supporting details are expected; inconsistent grammar mechanics including subordinating conjunctions and basic spelling; rushed test-taking behavior evidenced by significantly reduced time spent on Tests 2 and 3 (17m and 23m vs 29m on Test 1) leading to incomplete responses.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign paragraph structure KGs focusing on topic sentences, supporting details, and concluding sentences with guided practice before retesting Q11-style prompts. Practice subordinating conjunctions and sentence combining exercises to address grammar mechanics gaps. Implement timed practice sessions with emphasis on pacing strategies to ensure adequate time for thorough responses rather than rushed completion.

analysis.questions_missed: Q11 (paragraph writing), Q1-Q5 (sentence construction and grammar), Q7-Q9 (single sentence responses)
analysis.error_analysis: DaveyP consistently struggles with sentence construction tasks, particularly appositives, sentence combining, and word unscrambling, often creating grammatically incorrect or incomplete structures. In paragraph writing (Q11), he writes underdeveloped responses that lack sufficient detail and evidence from source material, though he shows improvement over time from 60% to 90%. His single-sentence response questions suffer from comma splices, missing articles, and punctuation errors.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot properly construct appositive phrases and combined sentences, resulting in fragmented or run-on constructions. Lacks understanding of complete sentence requirements including proper article usage ("I child" instead of "A child") and punctuation placement. Struggles with reading comprehension integration, often writing general responses without incorporating specific textual evidence as required by prompts.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G3-G4 KGs focusing specifically on appositives, sentence combining, and fragment correction with guided practice. Implement structured paragraph templates requiring students to cite 2-3 specific details from passages before writing responses. Practice daily sentence construction exercises emphasizing articles, punctuation, and complete sentence structure before attempting longer writing tasks.

skills.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.error_analysis: Elias consistently struggles with sentence combining tasks, often adding unnecessary conjunctions or failing to follow specific structural requirements (like using "and" with "because" or writing "Over" instead of conjunctive words). He consistently fails extended writing tasks (Q11), providing minimal responses like "He liked pokemon" instead of well-organized paragraphs, and shows confusion with basic grammatical concepts like appositives, where he adds descriptive words instead of creating proper appositive phrases.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot construct complex sentence structures including appositives, conjunctive combinations, and lacks understanding of sentence types and transitions. Fails to develop extended written responses beyond single sentences, showing inability to organize and elaborate ideas into paragraph format. Demonstrates inconsistent subject-verb agreement and struggles with identifying appropriate connecting words and conjunctions for sentence combining tasks.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G3-G4 KGs focusing specifically on appositives, conjunctions, and sentence combining with structured practice before complex tasks. Implement paragraph development exercises starting with 3-4 sentence responses using graphic organizers and sentence starters before attempting full paragraph writing. Practice extended writing tasks with scaffolded prompts that break down the Q11-style questions into smaller, manageable parts with explicit instruction on topic sentences and supporting details.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Animated movies are fun to watch, but they also teach several important life lessons. Many animated movies show characters learning about mistakes, kindness, and teamwork. These lessons help kids understand how to act in real life. 




First, animated movies teach children that making mistakes is a part of growing. In many stories, characters face problems and have to try again even if their spirits are low. This teaches the viewers that it is okay to fail as long as they keep trying.




Animated films also teach kindness and honesty. Characters often help their friends or tell the truth even when it is hard. These moments help children learn the difference bewtween right and wrong. 




Another lesson in animated movies is teamwork and friendship. Characters work together to solve problems and support each other. This shows children how important it is to cooperate with others.




Overall, animated movies are more than just entertainment. They teach lessons about mistakes, kindness, and teamwork. Because of this, animated films can help children learn valuable life skills.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
When Carter Steadman turned nine, he didn't want toys for his birthday. Instead, Carter wanted to build a small garden where butterflies could rest, eat food, and lay eggs. He cleared part of his backyard and planted rows of milkweed, which is the monarchs' favorite food. Now all he had to do was wait. 




Did you know every fall millions of monarch butterflies fly thousands of miles south from Canada to the mountains in mexico. They spend the cold months of the year resting in trees and conserving energy. When warmer mounths arrive, new generations of the butterflies begin the long journey back home, stopping only to eat and lay eggs. Monarch migration is one of the most remarkable and intresting migrations in nature.




In comtemporary times, sadly, fewer butterflies have been completeing this long journey. Humans have cleared land for farms, in the process destroying milkweed plants that monarchs rely on. More hurricanes and tornados have also damaged the forests where butterflies spend their winter. Many Scientists are nervous that the monarch population could disapear completely.




To help the monarchs, thousands of people across North America have learned to become citizen scientists. These citizen scientists tag butterflies with tiny stickers, count caterpillars, and record monarch butterfly sightings. These reports help real scientists know how the butterflies are doing across the country and where they need protection most. 




Carter continues to raise and tag these remarkable butterflies in his backyard. Carter belives that even small actions can result in a big difference. Thanks to these wonderful citizen scientists like Carter, monarch butterflies may have a much higher chance to survive.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how students learn new languages in effective ways. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
At a busy local café, people speak a variety of languages. At one table a family talk in spanish. At the coffee counter, a worker was singing along to a french song on the radio. Scenes like this happen everyday all over the United States, a country built by immigrants. English may be the most common school language, millions of americans grew up using two languages at home.




Knowing a second language is helpful in a multitude of ways. Many studies show it can can strengthen memory and help develop problem-solving skills. Also, Scientists say young childrens' brains are still devolping so they learn new sounds easier. This means giving young children an early start can help them in learning another language.




A good way to help kids learn a new language is through immersion. In an immersion school or class, teachers use the chosen language all day. Students hear it while talking to friends, in lessons and even small requests, like asking to go to the bathroom. Because they use use the language daily, students pick up the phrases and grammer quickly. 




Another way to learn is real life communication with a native speaker. Certain classrooms work hard to connect their students with pen pals who speak they language they are learning. These young children write letters or emails to share stories about their daily lives. These connections help to motivate students to pratice there language and show that learning a language has a purpose. 




Scientists and experts still study how a young bran affects language learning. A few experts say older students learn better in structered classes. However, others belive youger children learn natrually because they copy the sound and accents around them more easily. No matter how people learn the language, it can help open many doors. In  conclusion, learning a language young can help kids connect with many people and explore the world with much confidence.

analysis.error_analysis: Evelyn consistently struggles with constructing appositives, instead using conjunctions like "so" when asked to combine sentences with appositives. She demonstrates persistent spelling errors ("becouse," "exited," "acheve") and frequently writes single sentences or underdeveloped responses for paragraph prompts that require extended writing with evidence from text.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot distinguish between appositives and other sentence combining methods (uses conjunctions instead of descriptive phrases); writes minimal responses to extended writing prompts without developing ideas or providing sufficient textual evidence; demonstrates inconsistent spelling of common words and difficulty with apostrophes in contractions.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G4 KGs specifically targeting appositive construction and identification before conjunction use; practice structured paragraph writing with graphic organizers that require topic sentence, supporting details, and evidence before retesting; implement daily spelling practice focusing on frequently misspelled words ("because," "excited," "achieve") and contraction rules.

Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.error_analysis: The student consistently struggles with expository essay writing, scoring only 70-75% on Q11 across all three tests despite the essays being worth 75% of the total test points. The student also missed a basic grammar question about "because" usage in Test 2, indicating fundamental sentence construction issues. Time management is inconsistent, with two tests completed in rushed conditions (22m and 48m) while one took an excessive 94+ hours.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot construct well-developed expository essays with proper thesis statements and body paragraph structure as required by the prompts. Lacks proficiency in basic subordinating conjunction usage (specifically "because" for causal relationships). Struggles with time management and pacing during writing assessments, either rushing through or taking excessively long periods.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign expository writing KGs focusing on thesis development and body paragraph structure with topic sentences before retesting. Practice timed writing exercises with 45-60 minute limits to develop appropriate pacing skills. Review subordinating conjunctions and sentence combining activities, particularly causal relationship words like "because," "since," and "due to."

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Many people think that animated kids movies are purely for entertainment, but this is not true. These child shows and movies actually teach important life lessons that kids should learn early on in life.




In a countless amount of programs, the main characters have to solve big problms. Such a losing something that they love or making a mistake that they have to fix. Children see this and realize that it is okay to make mistakes and that mistakes are just a part of learning.




Animated programs also display a great variety of values. These values include teamwork, friendship, listening, forgiveness, and learning how to tell the truth even when it is hard.




Finally, kids shows often include lots of different cultures in them. This helps children accept different people, places, and ideas. It also educates them more about the world.




Even though movies cant replace lessons you learn in real life from parents and teachers. Watching stories about bravery, kindness, and hope can make children think about how thwy act in their own lives.2c:T8b6,

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
25 million. That is the number of monarch butterflies that die every year during migration. Monarch butterflies face countless challenges every single year, just for a chance to survive next year. Humans can help stop that, there are so many different ways that humans can help as well.




First of all, you can tag the the butterflies. This helps the government get an idea of how many butterflies are left. People who do this are often reffered to as "citizen scientists."




You can also help by building a station for them. At these stations, the butterflies can rest lay eggs and much more. This is a very important thing that citizen scientist can do in order to help the monarchs.




Lastly, there is planting/making gardens. All you have to do is clear a small space in your backyard and then plant some milkweed, which are the butterflies' favorite food. After that you just wait for the butterflies to come.




All in all, humans, play a big part in caring for monarch butterflies, and will continue to do so. Monarchs are becoming very endangered, so if you ever see one, just appreciate it because it might be the last time.2c:Tb65,

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how students learn new languages in effective ways. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
There are many different ways to learn a language. Most people in the world grow up speaking two languages. Languages can be learned through structured lessons, learning them as a child and just being around them all the time, and the final method is through Pen Pal systems.




First of all, some scientists believe that the best way to learn dialects is by structured lessons. This method is usually very strenuous and takes quite a long time. This method also utilizes real-time communication to strenghthen knowledge.




The second method is how most people learn languages, and that is through just being around them as a kid. When a child's mind is developing, their brains are more open to new sounds and accents which helps them with the base of their language.




Lastly, there is having a Pen Pal. This is a method where two people send messages to each other in whatevr language they are trying to learn. It helps with  critical thinking as well as just getting to know the language overall.




All in all, there are many different ways to learn languages, and each is easy enough to put into action. So if you ever are trying to learn a new language, I reccomend giving some of these methods a try.2c:Td9d,

skills.skill: Identify Topic Sentence And Sequence Details

analysis.error_analysis: Harper consistently struggles with appositive construction, repeatedly adding information with conjunctions ("and") instead of creating proper appositives as demonstrated across Q1 and Q3 responses. The student also writes minimal, underdeveloped paragraphs for Q11 extended responses, often providing only 1-2 sentences when a full paragraph is required. Additionally, Harper shows persistent spelling errors throughout all tests (e.g., "restaraunts," "becasue," "sighn," "souvenier").
analysis.root_causes: Cannot distinguish between appositives and compound sentences - consistently uses coordinating conjunctions instead of embedding descriptive phrases. Lacks understanding of paragraph structure and development requirements for extended writing tasks. Has not mastered basic spelling patterns for common words, indicating weak phonetic encoding skills.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G4 KGs specifically targeting appositive phrase construction with direct instruction on embedding vs. joining techniques. Provide structured paragraph templates for Q11 responses with explicit requirements for topic sentences, supporting details, and conclusion before retesting. Implement daily spelling practice focusing on high-frequency words the student consistently misspells, using multisensory encoding strategies.

analysis.error_analysis: Ian consistently struggles with extended writing tasks, particularly Q11 paragraph responses where he either writes completely off-topic content (like the taco paragraph) or provides minimal, underdeveloped responses that lack text evidence and organization. His sentence combining and revision tasks show frequent grammatical errors including incorrect comma placement, subject-verb disagreement, and misunderstanding of structural requirements like appositives and exclamatory sentences.
analysis.root_causes: Inability to construct well-organized paragraphs with topic sentences, supporting details, and conclusions; lack of understanding of sentence combining techniques using coordinating conjunctions while maintaining subject-verb agreement; poor comprehension of how to incorporate text evidence into written responses rather than writing personal opinions or unrelated content.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Paragraph Writing G4 KGs focusing on paragraph structure with guided templates before attempting any extended response questions; practice Sentences G4 KGs specifically targeting compound sentence formation and subject-verb agreement in complex constructions; implement reading comprehension activities that require students to identify and paraphrase text evidence before incorporating it into written responses.

analysis.questions_missed: Q11 (essay questions), Q3, Q8 (multiple choice questions on sentence structure/elaboration)
analysis.error_analysis: Kai consistently struggles with essay completion, showing incomplete responses across all tests with essays ending mid-sentence or containing minimal content. The student also demonstrates difficulty with sentence combining and elaboration techniques in multiple choice questions. Technical issues appear present (responses showing "$31" instead of actual text), but the pattern of incomplete essays suggests time management and content development challenges.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot complete full-length expository essays within time constraints - consistently produces incomplete responses that end abruptly mid-sentence. Lacks understanding of logical conjunctions and sentence elaboration techniques as evidenced by incorrect multiple choice answers. Struggles with sustained writing stamina and organizing sufficient content to meet the 3-body paragraph requirement.
analysis.recommended_actions: Practice timed essay writing with structured outlines to improve completion rates and time management skills. Assign specific instruction on coordinating conjunctions and sentence combining techniques to address multiple choice errors. Implement scaffolded paragraph development exercises focusing on expanding single ideas into full paragraphs before attempting complete essays.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Animated movies can help teach children many important life lessons.


One of those important life lessons is kindness. In many movies, characters show kindness throughout the film and have to support other people. Watching these movies can help kids learn how to be kind to other people.


Another important life lessons is bravery. Frequently in movies, characters have to fight evil villans and overcome many different challenges. These movies can motivate children to be brave and stick up for themselves and other people.


Lastly, another important life lesson that is common in movies is teamwork. Characters in the movie have to work together to overcome a goal or to support other people in hard times. By watching this, kids will learn to be more supportive of others and may get better at working with other people.


In conclusion, movies have many life skills that can help children in their life.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
People are helping monarch butterflies survive through many different ways. 




There are many different ways that people can help monarch butterflies, and one of the most common ways is tagging butterflies. People can tag butterflies, count caterpillars, and record monarch sightings online. This can help scientists understand how butterflies are doing and when they need protection.




Another way that people help are way stations. These small gardens are places that monarchs can rest and lay eggs, and also has milkweed, the butterflies' favorite food. This helps the butterflies have more energy and can also help their offspring.




Lastly, there is also planting different plants and fighting against deforestation. Butterflies need land to grow and stay safe, and humans are taking a lot of that land. Planting and fighting against deforestation can help there be more land where the butterflies can eat and survive.




In conclusion, there are many different ways that people are helping butterflies, even if the problem is still humans.32:Tb65,

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how students learn new languages in effective ways. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
There are many effective ways that students can learn new language, which can help people in many ways.




One way children learn new languages is through immersion. Immersion classes use that new language all day, making it so that they learn new phrases throughout the day. They also learn good grammar by listening to how the language sounds. 




Another helpful method is real communication. Connecting students with pen pals in other countries can help them improve their writing skills and motivate them to practice the language. Real communication can show the student that language learning has a purpose.




Regardless of the method, learning a second language can help people in many ways and can strengthen memory and improve problem-solving skills. Although it is still being researched how age affects language learning, it is obvious that it helps people connect with others and explore the world with confidence.




All in all, because there are many language learning methods, learning a language can be easier for some people and helps people grow and develop.32:Td9d,

questions.title: Extended Text Question
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how teamwork helped restore the Mithi River and what the community learned from this experience. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : U
questions.student_answer:
Teamwork helped people restore the Mithi River and the community learned many lessons from the cleanup.

At first, the river was full of trash and not many people visited it. The river was black from all of the sewage and was very dirty, so a group called Earth5R decided to help the river become cleaner.

People worked together to map out the dirtiest parts of the river and test the water. They held cleanup events and worked with scientists. They planted plants to help clean the water and prevent soil from washing away.

Soon the river started looking better and people started coming back. This shows that teamwork and taking action can help, you shouldn't wait for other people to do it for you, and that small efforts add up over time.

All in all, this shows that working together and helping others can help communities and that small actions can lead to big results.

analysis.error_analysis: Kanhai consistently struggles with sentence combining and appositive construction, often making grammatical errors or creating awkward phrasings when asked to merge sentences or add descriptive phrases. His paragraph writing shows good content knowledge but suffers from mechanical errors, spelling mistakes, and incomplete responses, particularly evident in the nonsensical response in Test 4 Q11 and the cut-off response in Test 5 Q11.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot reliably construct grammatically correct appositives and combined sentences, frequently making subject-verb agreement errors and awkward word choices. Struggles with time management and focus during longer writing tasks, leading to incomplete or incoherent responses on major paragraph questions. Makes consistent spelling and mechanical errors that interfere with clarity of written communication.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G4-G5 KGs focusing specifically on appositive construction and sentence combining with coordinating conjunctions. Practice timed paragraph writing with structured outlines to improve focus and completion rates on extended responses. Implement spelling and proofreading checklists for all written work to address mechanical errors before retesting.

analysis.error_analysis: Lincoln consistently struggles with grammar mechanics, particularly spelling errors (brocken/broken, sprakling/sparkling, momment/moment, exept/except) and sentence construction issues. He fails to understand specific grammatical concepts like appositives (adding "awaiting" instead of a true appositive phrase) and struggles with conjunction selection. His responses often lack the depth and development expected, particularly in shorter analytical responses where he provides overly simple explanations.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot construct proper appositives or identify appropriate conjunctions for sentence relationships. Demonstrates inconsistent spelling of common words and struggles with precise word choice in descriptive writing. Provides underdeveloped responses to analytical questions, often giving surface-level explanations without sufficient detail or reasoning.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Grammar G3 KGs focusing specifically on appositive construction and conjunction usage with targeted practice exercises. Implement spelling reinforcement activities for commonly misspelled words, particularly those with double letters and common phonetic patterns. Practice analytical response development using structured templates that require students to provide reasoning and evidence beyond single-clause answers.

analysis.questions_missed: Q1/Q3 (appositive construction), Q5 (sentence completion/conjunctions), Q7/Q8/Q9 (single sentence responses), Q11 (paragraph writing)
analysis.error_analysis: The student consistently fails to construct appositives correctly, instead writing compound sentences or adding unrelated phrases. In single-sentence response questions, the student often omits necessary punctuation (commas in complex sentences) and struggles with spelling accuracy. For paragraph writing, the student demonstrates good content understanding but produces responses that lack proper organization and contain multiple spelling/grammar errors.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot identify or construct appositive phrases - confuses them with compound sentences or descriptive additions. Inconsistent punctuation usage, particularly with comma placement in complex sentences and conditional statements. Spelling difficulties with common words ("immidately," "obsesson," "Sence") that impact overall writing quality despite strong content comprehension.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Grammar G3 KGs specifically targeting appositive construction with direct instruction and practice exercises. Focus on punctuation rules for complex sentences through targeted drills before attempting longer responses. Implement spelling intervention using high-frequency word lists and provide spell-check strategies during writing assignments to improve accuracy while maintaining content quality.

analysis.questions_missed: Q11 (essay questions consistently scoring 70-80%), Q3 and Q5 (multiple choice questions on sentence combining and expansion)
analysis.error_analysis: Lorenzo consistently rushes through tests (17-36 minutes) and demonstrates incomplete essay responses, as evidenced by his Test 4 essay ending mid-sentence. His multiple choice errors on Q3 and Q5 show difficulty with sentence combining using conjunctions and expanding basic sentences with specific details. The essay responses show basic structure understanding but lack full development and completion.
analysis.root_causes: Time management issues causing rushed completion and incomplete responses; inability to expand sentences with appropriate conjunctions and specific details; difficulty sustaining focus to complete extended writing tasks, resulting in abrupt endings and underdeveloped paragraphs.
analysis.recommended_actions: Implement timed writing practice sessions starting with 25-30 minutes to build stamina and completion habits; assign Sentence Structure KGs focusing on conjunctions, sentence combining, and kernel expansion exercises; practice paragraph completion drills where Lorenzo must finish partially written essays to develop sustained writing endurance.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Animated movies can teach valuable lessons to young children. They are an entertaining and fun way to learn these fundamental lessons. 




Movies can teach kids about the importance of friendship and teamwork. The main character has many friends and they all work together. Like in the show Elsa, the characters work together to navigate the forest to find Elsa.




Movies can also teach about temperance and controlling your emotions. The characters must learn to overcome negative thoughts. Going back to Elsa, she has to control her thoughts and emotions so the ice won't go out of control.




Lastly, movies can teach about bravery and determination. It's about working hard and doing things no matter how scary. In Elsa, the characters must use bravery because the forest is very scary and dangerous.




In conclusion, animated movies can teach many lessons such as teamwork, controlling emotions, and bravery. While parent and teacher guidance can't be replaced, movies can offer help.2c:T8b6,

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
Many people in North America are making an effort to help the monarchs during the monarchs' migration. While us humans are creating some problems for the monarchs, many people want to protect the monarchs.




Some people are builiding waypoints in their backyards. Their waypoints usually have milkweed, which is a vital source of food for the monarchs. Waypoints also have areas for the butterflies to rest and breed. Waypoints can help monarchs during their migration. 




Several people also are volunteering to be citizen scientists. They conduct reasearch like tagging butterflies. They also count caterpillars. They also record monarch sightings.




We have been destroying valuable things the monarchs need. We have been clearing out milkweed fields to contsruct houses and farms. We are damaging forests where the butterflies live in for the winter. Many people acknoledge this, and they are trying to stop it.




Monarchs are in difficult times now, but they have better odds now that people are trying to help the situation. They have increasing chances of surving. Best of all, we may get view their beautiful migrations for years to come.2c:Tb65,

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how students learn new languages in effective ways. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
Learning a new language at young age can be very beneficial, and there are multiple ways to learn a new language. Speaking multiple languages can strengthen memory and problem-solving skills. Children can learn new languages quicker because their minds are still growing. 




Immersion classes is one way to learn a new language. Students use the language they are learning for communication in an immersion class. Immersion can help students learn by exposing them to new vocabulary.




Real communication is also a great way to learn another language. Students get to write to other people in different countries. By connecting to other people, students have a fun way to learn a new language. 




There are many kid-friendly apps that can help children learn a new language. While these apps cannot replace traditional language learning, many kids can benefit from these online tools. These apps are usually easily accesible and quicken the speed of language learning.




There are many ways to learn a language. You take immersion classes, you can use real communication, you can have learning apps, or you can do all three. But no matter the method, learning a new language is always beneficial, and it can even be fun.


2c:Td9d,

questions.title: Extended Text Question
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how teamwork helped restore the Mithi River and what the community learned from this experience. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : U
questions.student_answer:
The Mithi river used to be a disgutsing, fithy river that nobody liked. However, that has changed thanks to a group called Earth5R. Now the river is clean and the communities that helped learned some valuable lessons. 

The group started by mapping the river, and testing the water. They started to clean up by inviting people to help remove trash from the river. Soon, the whole community was helping with the project. 

Earth5R also did more than just removing trash. They planted fresh grasses to filter the water and restore the river to its clean state. They also recycled all the garbage they found to better benefit the communtiy. 

At last, their efforts proved successful. The river was cleaner and people went back to using it. People even continued to mantain the river. 

The community learned a lot from this experience. They learned that when they come together they can make a big impact. They also learned the importance of not ignoring and protecting natural environoments.

analysis.error_analysis: Lukas consistently struggles with sentence mechanics, particularly combining sentences with specific conjunctions (uses "so" instead of "since") and converting sentence types (adds "Did" but keeps declarative verb form "fell"). His paragraph responses lack development and organization - he writes surface-level observations without fully addressing prompts or providing detailed explanations as required.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot properly manipulate sentence structures when given specific grammatical requirements (subordinating conjunctions, interrogative transformations). Writes underdeveloped paragraphs that list facts rather than explaining relationships or providing thorough analysis. Persistent spelling errors indicate weak phonetic encoding skills affecting written expression quality.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Grammar G3 KGs focusing on subordinating conjunctions and sentence type transformations with guided practice. Practice structured paragraph writing using graphic organizers that require explanation of cause-effect relationships before attempting full responses. Implement systematic spelling intervention targeting high-frequency words and phonetic patterns observed in errors.

analysis.error_analysis: Marcus consistently struggles with sentence construction and following specific prompt instructions. He frequently ignores required conjunctions (using "when" instead of "since"), writes incomplete or grammatically incorrect sentences, and fails to directly answer questions as asked. His responses often contain spelling errors and missing punctuation, particularly apostrophes in contractions.
analysis.root_causes: Cannot follow specific grammatical instructions (fails to use required conjunctions, sentence types); struggles with sentence completeness and clarity (fragments, run-ons, unclear pronoun references); consistently misspells common words and omits essential punctuation, particularly apostrophes and commas.
analysis.recommended_actions: Assign Sentences G3-G4 KGs focusing on subordinating conjunctions, sentence types, and combining sentences with specific connectors. Practice structured sentence writing with emphasis on following exact prompt requirements before attempting longer responses. Implement targeted spelling and punctuation drills for high-frequency words and apostrophe usage in contractions.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how animated movies can teach children important life lessons. expository essay Use information from the passage to support your ideas. : Make sure your response includes the following : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it : Use transitions, keep your ideas in
questions.student_answer:
Animated movies can teach children important life lessons. To start off, many animated movies incorporate the power of friendship and teamwork. Additionally, they usually have a lesson that shows how making mistakes is how you learn. Lastly, they show how honesty is best. 

  First, some movies show how using friendship and teamwork is the best way to problem solve. They usually show a character who is having trouble doing something. The character will probably feel frustrated and give up. Finally, the characters friends will show up and they will all solve the problem. These movies show that friendship and teamwork are important.

  Additionally, some movies show that making mistakes are how you learn. They will show a character making a mistake and feeling frustrated about it. Then the character will usually be told by a parent or teacher that making mistakes is how you learn and that everyone makes mistakes. The character will then realise that making misakes is okay. 

  Finally, many movies show how honesty is best. A character will make a mistake that they are ashamed of. They will then try to hide the mistake by telling multiple lies to their parents or peers. Their parents or peers will eventually figure out that the character was lying. The character would the realise that being honest is better than hiding a mistake. 

  By reading this information, you now know how animated movies teach children important life lessons. They can show how teamork is important, how making mistakes is how you learn, and how being honest is best.

questions.title: Essay with Rich Text
questions.prompt: Write an  explaining how people are helping monarch butterflies survive. expository essay  Use information from the passage to support your ideas.   : Make sure your response includes the following  : Introduction with a strong thesis, 3 body paragraphs with topic sentences, and a conclusion (5 points) Write 5 clear paragraphs   : Support each point with facts or examples from the passage and explain what they show (5 points) Use evidence and explain it   : Use transitions, keep your ideas in or
questions.student_answer:
People are helping monarch butterfiles, beautiful creatures, survive. They record monarchs they see online, tag them, and count caterpillars. All of these actions help save monarch butterflies little by little. 

   The first thing people do to save the monarchs is recording the ones they see online. If someone was outside and they saw a monarch they would take a photo and post it online. This helps scientists with their research. 

   The next thing people do to help the monarchs survive is they tag the butterflies. If someone sees a monarch in the wild, they will put a small sticker on them. This helps people know to keep them safe.

   Lastly, people count caterpillars to help save the monarchs. When someone spots a caterpillar, they will add another caterpillar to their list. This will help people see how many caterpillars turn into butterflies. 

   After reading this essay, you now know how many people try and save monarch butterflies. They tag monarchs, record the monarchs they see online, and they count the caterpillars they see. Every person that helps is saving multiple monarchs. 2c:Tb65,
